page: Microsoft Azure E192is_v6 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Esv6 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and features an x86_64 architecture with 192 dedicated vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 1832.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 9.54 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage, though the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable. There are no GPUs bundled with this configuration, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or parallel computing tasks that require dedicated accelerators. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its isolated nature and massive memory footprint, the E192is_v6 offers high resource density for enterprise workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation and isolated hardware profile provide predictable performance and security boundaries, which is qualitatively cost-efficient for large-scale, memory-intensive applications. This server is designed for workloads such as large relational databases, in-memory analytics, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E192is_v6 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 192 dedicated vCPUs and 1832.0 GB of system memory, providing 9.54 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, and it contains no GPUs. Designed for high-density memory requirements, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance. The isolated hardware profile makes it highly suitable for secure, large-scale enterprise workloads, such as massive in-memory databases, data warehousing, and complex enterprise resource planning systems that demand significant memory capacity and strict resource isolation.
